method: Identified a scale mismatch in the Masked Language Model (MLM) head of Learned Sparse Retrieval (LSR) models (such as SPLADE) when using stronger pretrained encoders, leading to performance degradation and training collapse.
Proposed a simple initialization-time correction that rescales the MLM-head projection by a constant factor (k) before SPLADE training.

This zero-cost adjustment improves training stability without modifying the model architecture or training objective, preserves the direction and lexical information, introduces no additional parameters, and adds no training or inference cost.
datasets: BEIR-13 (for evaluating SPLADE models across in-domain and out-of-domain retrieval benchmarks)
limitations: Under standard SPLADE training recipes, stronger pretrained encoders with large MLM-head L2 norms (e.g., RoBERTa, ModernBERT, Ettin) suffer performance degradation and training collapse.
This failure is due to a scale mismatch in the MLM head, where an inflated scale can amplify sparse activations, distort matching scores, and destabilize contrastive training.
The bottleneck in adapting pretrained encoders to LSR is the calibration of the MLM-head scale, not merely encoder capacity.
